title: Non-AW command-generation fixture package

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Add a small non-AW command package fixture that proves command-generation works without AW names, runtime modules, or repository layout assumptions.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep command-generation as a maintainer dependency only; AW remains the owner of AW command meaning, generated outputs, and runtime-boundary policy.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Implementation order, helper names, and validation tightening inside this lane.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The lane needs command semantic drift, runtime dependency drift, or files outside the declared owner surfaces.
canonical_core.next_action: Create the fixture package, render it through public APIs, and add conformance/freshness tests for portable and custom primitive cases.
completion_criteria: The non-AW fixture renders through public command-generation APIs.
Its generated Python command package runs at least one portable operation.
A custom primitive is implemented or reports a clear unsupported error.
Tests fail if the fixture imports AW modules, paths, or command names.

goal: Add a small non-AW command package fixture that proves command-generation works without AW names, runtime modules, or repository layout assumptions.
non_goals: Do not build a polished product example.
Do not require full TypeScript target parity.

intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: no, it completes one child lane

execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing command semantics, generated package layout, runtime dependency policy, or unrelated package internals.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated AW runtime behavior, unrelated package internals, or generated outputs not covered by freshness proof.

post_decomposition_delegation.reason: The current branch spans two repos and active PR context; hand off only after a lane becomes isolated and mechanical.

adaptive_assurance.reason: The epic changes ownership and dependency boundaries across AW and command-generation.

active_milestone.scope: Add a small non-AW command package fixture that proves command-generation works without AW names, runtime modules, or repository layout assumptions.
active_milestone.ready: ready after dependency lanes complete: command-generation-public-api-host-manifest, command-generation-primitive-registry

immediate_next_action: Create the fixture package, render it through public APIs, and add conformance/freshness tests for portable and custom primitive cases.
blockers: None currently known; dependency order is recorded in the decomposition.
invariants: Do not add command-generation to AW runtime dependencies.
Do not hide AW-specific behavior inside generic command_generation renderers.
Generated package proof remains the freshness and target-proof authority.
completion_criteria: The non-AW fixture renders through public command-generation APIs.
Its generated Python command package runs at least one portable operation.
A custom primitive is implemented or reports a clear unsupported error.
Tests fail if the fixture imports AW modules, paths, or command names.
proof_expectations: Package tests render and execute the fixture through public APIs.
Fixture static checks prove no AW imports, paths, or command names are required.

execution_run.what happened: planning scaffold tightened before implementation continuation
execution_run.result for continuation: execute only when dependencies are satisfied
execution_run.next step: Create the fixture package, render it through public APIs, and add conformance/freshness tests for portable and custom primitive cases.

proof_report.evidence for proof achieved state: Registered execplan plus parent decomposition reference.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: No lane is closed by planning alone; implementation proof must satisfy the issue criteria.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Any acceptance criterion is missing, validation fails, or command-generation becomes an AW runtime dependency.

signals found: The agent started direct implementation of an eight-issue epic before promoting the roadmap candidates into decomposition and execplan surfaces.
signals routed: GitHub #1215 tracks the epic candidate-lane promotion guardrail discovered during dogfooding.
